title: [Bug]: Payload normalization accepts non-standard JSON constants
summary: Payload normalization uses Python JSON parsing defaults, which accept non-standard constants such as NaN and Infinity. Those values are not valid interoperable JSON and can later be written to JSON-capable sinks as invalid JSON.
observed: A payload such as {"value": NaN} can be treated as valid JSON instead of being rejected by json_only mode or wrapped as text by json_or_envelope mode.
expected: Only standards-compliant JSON should be preserved as JSON. Non-standard constants should be treated as invalid JSON: json_only mode should raise SerializationError, and json_or_envelope mode should preserve the original UTF-8 text in the nats-sinks payload envelope.
reproduction: Run a focused regression test that normalizes a payload containing NaN and asserts json_only rejects it while json_or_envelope wraps it as text.
failing_test: Add a focused regression test under tests/unit that proves non-standard JSON constants are not accepted as valid payload JSON.
impact: This can affect any JSON-capable sink, including Oracle and file output, because invalid non-finite values may become persisted JSON text that downstream tools reject or interpret differently.
delivery_semantics: Commit-then-ACK ordering must not change. The fix only changes payload classification at the serialization boundary: invalid standard JSON remains eligible for DLQ in json_only mode and durable text-envelope storage in json_or_envelope mode.
security: The fix must not log raw payload bodies. Error messages may include the subject but must not echo the invalid payload content.
acceptance: A focused regression test covers NaN payload handling.
json_only mode raises SerializationError for non-standard JSON constants.
json_or_envelope mode wraps non-standard JSON constants as UTF-8 text.
NatsEnvelope.payload_as_json raises SerializationError for non-standard JSON constants.
Oracle and file sink JSON serialization remains standards-compliant.
Existing unit, smoke, documentation, package, and release checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, payload normalization tests, envelope tests, Oracle mapping tests, file sink tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and payload documentation to explain that nats-sinks uses standards-compliant JSON parsing and treats non-standard constants as invalid JSON.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
